title: delphinium consolida subsp. pubescens
intro: Delphinium consolida subsp. pubescens, also known as the hairy larkspur, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the delphinium genus. It is characterized by its tall spikes of purple flowers, which grow in clusters and have a distinctive shape. The hairy larkspur has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Herbal preparations of Delphinium consolida subsp. pubescens are often used to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to soothe digestive problems, including nausea and diarrhea.
